parsed_findings.chain_steps: Auth bypass (line 2 logic flaw) -> mass assignment (line 6) -> persist escalated privileges (line 7)
parsed_findings.impact: Full privilege escalation; any authenticated user can grant themselves admin or support roles.
parsed_findings.proof: PATCH /api/users/123 with body {"isAdmin":true} merges directly onto the user object before save.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Send PATCH /api/users/<own_id> with JSON body {"isAdmin":true,"role":"admin"} and observe privilege escalation.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Mass assignment via Object.assign allows attacker to overwrite any user field including isAdmin/isSupport/role.

parsed_findings.chain_steps: IDOR (line 2) -> arbitrary user lookup (line 5) -> mass assignment (line 6) -> data corruption or privilege escalation
parsed_findings.impact: Any authenticated user can modify any other user's record without restriction.
parsed_findings.proof: Line 2 reads 'req.user.id req.user.isSupport' which is a syntax/logic error that evaluates req.user.id as truthy, never validating ownership.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Authenticate as user A, send PATCH /api/users/<user_B_id> — check passes because req.user.id is truthy and req.user.isSupport is falsy.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: IDOR: the auth check compares req.user.id to nothing — missing equality check against req.params.id lets any user edit any account.

parsed_findings.chain_steps: No validation (line 6) -> Object.assign pollution -> persisted to DB (line 7)
parsed_findings.impact: Prototype pollution or silent overwrite of security-sensitive fields such as password hash or email.
parsed_findings.proof: req.body is merged directly with no schema validation, whitelist, or sanitization step.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Send PATCH with body {"__proto__":{"isAdmin":true}} or arbitrary fields like {"password":"attacker"} to overwrite sensitive properties.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: No input validation on req.body before merging; attacker can inject prototype-polluting or unexpected fields.

parsed_findings.chain_steps: 1) Inject payload in `name` 2) os.system executes injected shell commands 3) RCE achieved

parsed_findings.chain_steps: 1) Read src balance (line 5) in thread A and B concurrently -> 2) Both pass insufficient check (line 6) -> 3) Both write reduced balance (line 8) overwriting each other -> net loss exceeds real balance
parsed_findings.impact: Attacker drains account to negative balance, effectively stealing funds from the platform.
parsed_findings.proof: Two concurrent /transfer requests read the same src balance before either write commits, allowing both to pass the check and deduct from the same funds twice.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Send two simultaneous POST /transfer requests for the full balance; both read src before either write, both pass line 6, total debited exceeds actual balance.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Race condition (TOCTOU) between balance read and write enables double-spend.

parsed_findings.chain_steps: 1) amt = int(-500) -> 2) src(50) < -500 is False, passes check -> 3) set_balance(sender, 50-(-500)=550) -> 4) recipient loses 500
parsed_findings.impact: Attacker can steal funds from any target account by supplying a negative transfer amount.
parsed_findings.proof: int(request.json['amount']) with amt=-100 passes src < amt check (e.g. 50 < -100 is false), then credits attacker src+100 and debits recipient.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: POST /transfer with amount=-500; line 6 check passes, line 8 increases sender balance, line 9 decreases recipient balance.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: No validation that amount is positive; negative or zero values reverse or skip the transfer.

parsed_findings.chain_steps: 1) to = arbitrary ID from request -> 2) no existence/ownership check -> 3) db.set_balance writes to unvalidated account
parsed_findings.impact: Funds destroyed or injected into attacker-controlled ghost accounts; potential for financial inconsistency or privilege escalation.
parsed_findings.proof: db.get_balance(to) on a non-existent account likely returns 0 or creates a record, then db.set_balance writes attacker-controlled value to that ID with no ownership check.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: POST /transfer with to=999999 (non-existent user); funds are deducted from sender and credited to phantom account or cause an unhandled DB error.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Recipient account 'to' is never validated to exist or belong to a real user, enabling balance injection to arbitrary IDs.

parsed_findings.chain_steps: 1) Craft JWT with forged claims signed with wrong key → 2) catch block fires → 3) jwt.decode returns payload with no verification → 4) caller trusts returned object as authenticated identity
parsed_findings.impact: Full authentication bypass in production — attacker can forge any identity/claims.
parsed_findings.proof: jwt.decode() performs no signature check; an attacker submitting an unsigned or arbitrarily-signed token triggers the catch and gets a trusted decoded payload returned.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Send a Bearer token signed with 'none' algorithm or a wrong key; in production NODE_ENV the catch returns the unverified claims.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: JWT signature verification bypassed in production on any verify failure

parsed_findings.chain_steps: Exploit timing oracle to recover want -> craft matching got -> send arbitrary payload to process_event
parsed_findings.impact: Attacker can forge a valid HMAC signature, bypassing webhook authentication entirely.
parsed_findings.proof: got == want uses Python's short-circuit str equality, leaking signature validity through response time differences.

parsed_findings.chain_steps: Inverted null check → memcpy(NULL,...) → segfault or controlled write primitive
parsed_findings.impact: NULL pointer dereference leading to crash or memory corruption when out is NULL.
parsed_findings.proof: if (out || out_sz == 0) passes when out==NULL and out_sz>0, proceeding to memcpy(NULL,in,n).
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Call copy_name("x", NULL, 10); — guard passes, memcpy writes to NULL, crash/arbitrary write.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Guard condition is inverted: checks `out` (non-NULL) instead of `!out`, so null pointer is accepted and NULL dereference occurs on line 5-6.
